question:
Will a team from LPL (China) win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the region or country of the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of Worlds 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to Other.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia (https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page).

Teams counted toward each region:
- LCK (South Korea): Gen.G, T1, KT Rolster, Hanwha Life Esports
- LPL (China): Bilibili Gaming, Top Esports, Invictus Gaming, Anyone’s Legend
- LEC (Europe / EMEA): G2 Esports, Fnatic, Movistar KOI
- LTA (Americas): FlyQuest, 100 Thieves, Vivo Keyd Stars
- LCP (Pacific): PSG Talon, CTBC Flying Oyster, Team Secret Whales

evidences.title: LPL 2025 Regional Finals - Liquipedia League of Legends Wiki
evidences.content: ## Format [edit] Participants + Four teams with highest Championship Points besides the LPL Split 3 2025 Champion (as the region's 1st seed) and the team with the highest Championship Points (as the region's 2nd seed) will play. - If two teams have the same points, the team with higher Split 3 points will be ranked higher. Format + Four teams compete in a Double Elimination format. - Two teams with higher points begin in the Upper Bracket, the other two in the Lower Bracket. - Winner of the Upper Bracket qualifies for the World Championship as the region's 3rd seed. - Winner of the Lower Bracket qualifies for the World Championship as the region's 4th seed. + All matches are Bo5. + Click here for bracket's information in different circumstances [...] - The other two teams play in Quarter Final match. - Winner of the Grand final match qualifies for the World Championship as the region's 3rd seed. - Loser of the Grand final match qualifies for the World Championship as the region's 4th seed. If the region's team wins MSI and qualify for Worlds as region's 1st/2nd seed or If the region's team wins MSI but doesn't qualify for Split 3 Playoffs or If the region is the MSI runner-up re...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Format Explained: Everything You Need To Know
evidences.content: Each of these two regions will field a total of three teams in this tournament, just like the LEC. The LPL and the LCK, after being the top two regions at MSI, were awarded a fourth additional slot, although the two #4 seeds will have to battle it out in the play-in stage to qualify for the Swiss stage, the first official stage of Worlds 2025. According to the official schedule, the Swiss stage will kick off on October 15. ## Worlds 2025 Format is split into 3 stages ### Stage 1: Play-In Stage 2 Teams A single best-of-five series between LPL and LCK #4 seeds featuring Fearless Draft. Winners will advance to the Swiss stage, losers will be eliminated and go home early. ### Stage 2: Swiss Stage [...] ### Stage 2: Swiss Stage Introduced in 2023 to replace the group stage, the Swiss stage format has the main goal of creating higher-stakes matches and increase the cross-regional competition right from the start. 16 Teams The teams play up to five rounds. Each round, teams will play against an opponent with the same win-loss record. Matches are best-of-one until elimination or qualification matches (2 losses or 2 wins), which will then shift to best-of-threes. The 8 teams to get 3 win...

evidences.title: 2025 League of Legends World Championship qualification - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Appearance From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Qualification for the 2025 League of Legends World Championship occurred between July to September 2025, allocating seventeen (17) teams for the final tournament. The five Tier 1 regional leagues were expected to have a representation at the tournament. As the winner of the 2025 Mid-Season Invitational (MSI), Gen.G of the League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK) received an automatic slot in the tournament, provided it qualified for its league's playoffs; this was confirmed on 23 July 2025. By virtue of being the second best-performing region at MSI, China's League of Legends Pro League (LPL) also earned an additional slot to the World Championship. [...] ### Qualification via Regional Leagues [edit] In line with Riot Games' announcement of a new international tournament allocation per region beginning with the 2025 competitive season, each of the five regional leagues distributed three (3) quotas each to the best-performing teams, making a total of fifteen (15) berths. As qualification to Worlds was part of the regional league season, namely the third split, each league decided its own formats and qualification methods. The LPL wa...

evidences.title: All LoL Worlds Champions
evidences.content: South Korean teams (LCK) and Chinese teams (LPL) have dominated the championship in the past decade, with a lone European champion in the very first Worlds. ## Regional Dominance and Repeat Champions South Korea’s LCK is the most dominant region in Worlds history. Korean teams have won a total of 8 World Championships as of 2024. These title runs include a famous dynasty by SK Telecom T1 (now T1) and additional championships by Samsung’s teams and DAMWON Gaming. [...] Q: Who won LoL Worlds each year? A: The world champions for every year are listed above in our complete winners table. In brief: Fnatic won the inaugural World Championship in 2011; Taipei Assassins won in 2012; SK Telecom T1 (T1) dominated 2013, 2015, 2016; Samsung White took 2014; Samsung Galaxy won 2017; Invictus Gaming and FunPlus Phoenix brought home 2018 and 2019 respectively for China; DAMWON Gaming won 2020; Edward Gaming won 2021; DRX won 2022; and T1 were champions in 2023 and 2024. Q: Which team has the most LoL Worlds titles? A: South Korea’s T1 (formerly SK Telecom T1) holds the record with five World Championship titles. Q: Which region has won the most World Championships? A: Korea (LCK) is the leadi...

evidences.content: played in game one are banned for game two. The 20 champions in game 1 and two are banned for game three, and so on. For the Swiss stage, it works the same as it did in 2023 and 2024. The 16 teams are all given a seed based on how they performed in their region, with winners given the one seed, runner-ups the two seed, and third place the three seed. Depending on who wins the playin match, either the LCK or LPL3 seed will be promoted to two seed for this withdraw. This way, there are five one seeds, six two seeds, and five three seeds. In round one, one seeds will be drawn against three seeds, and two seeds will play against other two seeds in a best of one. The way Swiss works is this. If you win a match, you move up to 1 and 0. And if you lose, you go to 0 and one. Every team who has [...] become one to crown the world championship winner. If it works the same way as last year, the 3 and 0 teams will be put into pool one, the 3-1 teams in pool two and the 3 and2 teams in pool three. Then the 3-2 teams will be matched against a 3-0 team with the remaining 3 and 0 team playing against one of the 3-in1 teams. So for quarterfinals, it's better to have finished Swiss stage with the...

evidences.title: LoL: All Worlds Winners - Sheep Esports
evidences.content: Loading... Loading... The League of Legends World Championship, or Worlds, has seen 9 different organizations claim victory across its 14 editions. The LCK leads with an impressive 9 titles, while the LPL has won 3 times. Europe and the PCS have each secured 1 title. When it comes to individual achievements, Lee "Faker" Sang-hyeok stands as the most successful player, with a record 9 appearances at Worlds. Alongside his team, T1, he has claimed the trophy 5 times and reached 7 finals, making them the most crowned organization in the tournament's history. Fnatic, with 12 appearances by 2024, holds the record for most Worlds participations. Let’s dive into the competition’s history. ## 2011: Fnatic [...] MSIMSI Sheep Logo · · Sheep Esports - LoL: All Worlds Winners Loading... Loading... Loading... Loading... LOLWORLDS 28.03.25 - 14:4828.03.2025 - 14:48·10m10 minute(s) read· ByClément Chocat # LoL: All Worlds Winners This article chronicles every winner of the League of Legends World Championship from its inception in 2011 through 2024, highlighting each team's journey to victory, major upsets, historic milestones, and the evolving dominance of regions like South Korea’s LCK, China...

evidences.title: The Five Most Legendary Worlds Finals in League of Legends History | Esports Charts
evidences.content: The matchup was also a clash of regions’ pride: LCK vs. LPL, the dominant Korean squad versus a rising Chinese challenger. Hype was through the roof, with millions tuning in to watch this showdown live. In fact, the 2024 final hit a new record peak viewership of 6.9 million concurrent viewers (outside China), the highest in esports history. Faker claims a record fifth World Championship title / Photo by Riot Games [...] With that, EDG completed the upset 3:2, claiming their first-ever World Championship. The victory was monumental: not only did EDG bring the trophy back to China, but they also denied Korea a repeat title and became the third LPL team in history to lift the Cup. Chinese fans erupted in celebration: videos circulated of university students watching in dorms and then pouring into the streets to cheer. Even state media noted that about one in 30 people on the planet (mainly via Chinese streams) witnessed EDG’s victory live. The 2021 final is remembered for its gritty comeback and what it represented: an LCK juggernaut being toppled by the unlikeliest of LPL heroes. ## DRX vs. T1 (2022) [...] ## EDward Gaming vs. DWG KIA (2021) The 2021 final in Reykjavik, Iceland, f...

evidences.title: LoL - Worlds 2025: Top 20 players
evidences.content: ## 15. GALA (68) Undoubtedly the most important and decisive player of the year for Invictus Gaming, Chen "GALA" Wei is having arguably one of the best seasons of his career. He recorded two impressive pentakills during the Summer Split, including the first ever on the new champion Yunara. He led the LPL in average kills during Split 3, ranked second in DPM in the league, and maintained over 75% kill participation. The two-time MSI champion will now participate in his fifth consecutive Worlds, aiming for the one trophy that still eludes him. ## 16. Keria (52) [...] ## 4. Knight (176) Zhuo "Knight" Ding ranks fourth in our power ranking and is also the fourth player highlighted from Bilibili Gaming. The Chinese midlaner has been the most consistent performer for BLG this year, delivering a particularly strong Summer Split. He recorded the second-highest KDA among midlaners, secured the most kills per game, and led his team in damage share, underlining his crucial impact on BLG. Knight also claimed his sixth split title in the Summer, making him the most decorated player in LPL history. Having already won MSI, he now chases the only trophy that has eluded him, the Worlds. ## 5. Ta...

evidences.title: LoL Esports | League Handbook
evidences.content: ## League Handbook Everything you need to know about the Tier 1 professional League of Legends 2025 season. ##### About the Season LoL Esports is the game’s Tier 1 professional league, and is made up of five regions: Americas (LTA), EMEA (LEC), Korea (LCK), China (LPL), and Asia-Pacific (LCP). Over the course of the 2025 season, teams participate in a series of regional and international events that culminate at the final international event, Worlds, where the best team in the world is crowned. ##### Season Event Timeline - Split 1 REGIONAL event(Jan – Mar) The 2025 League of Legends Esports season kicks off with each region running their own Split format and style. The top team from each region qualifies for First Stand. - First Stand GLOBAL event(Mar) [...] GLOBAL event(Mar) The top team from each region's Split 1 come together for the first of three international events. The winning region will get a bye directly into the bracket stage of MSI. - Split 2 REGIONAL event(Mar – Jun) The second regional Split, with each region running their own Split format and style. The top two teams from each region qualify for MSI. - MSI GLOBAL event(Jun – Jul) The top two teams from each regi...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Heads to China! - LoL Esports
evidences.content: Worlds 2025 begins with 17 teams vying for the trophy. The Play-In Stage will kick off October 14 in Beijing at the state-of-the-art Beijing Smart Esports Center, a venue built specifically for premier esports events. This will be the first time the Beijing Smart Esports Center hosts a World Championship, and it promises to deliver an electric atmosphere. New this year, the Play-In Stage will host a one-day best of five between the fourth seeded team of the two best performing regions from MSI to punch their ticket to the Swiss Stage. From October 15-25, the Swiss Stage will bring together 16 teams from across the globe in a dynamic format, where every win moves you closer to knockout contention—and every loss brings you closer to elimination. [...] Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games. All Rights Reserved. Privacy NoticeTerms of ServiceAccessibility The 2025 League of Legends World Championship is heading back to China and will take place from October 14 to November 9. Fans around the world can look forward to world-class competition, unforgettable moments, and the crowning of the next World Champion. ### Play-In and Swiss Stages – Beijing Smart Esports Center, Beijing

evidences.title: LPL's history at the League of Legends World Championship - ESPN
evidences.content: Oh My God: 4th EDward Gaming: 5th-8th A year past and it's still pretty clear that LPL is the second best region in the world, as the elite continue to only fall to the South Korean elite. The buzz going around internationally was that EDward Gaming was very much in contention for the title, being the fusion of World Elite players, Clearlove and Fzzf, and newer players Ceng "U" Long and Tong "Koro1" Yang. All that was centered around the man that carried Peng "Aluka" Zhen-Ming and Positive Energy to an LPL title, the elite AD carry Zhu "NaMei" Jia-Wen. [...] OMG and SHRC were a little more lukewarm in terms of expectations, as both had clear synergy issues during the regular season. However, both still packed the same punch in terms of talent, with OMG still sporting its star players from the years past and Royal Club still having the eccentric AD, Uzi. LPL performed admirably yet again as all teams were able to make it out of the group stages, but EDG in particular looked shaky, having to go into a tiebreaker game against Taiwanese underdog, ahq e-Sports Club. [...] LGD was largely seen as favorites to take the whole tournament. Its all-star roster, save for weak-link jungler Z...

evidences.content: Gumayushi, Vi for owner, and Yona for Faker along with an additional skin for the MVP with that being prestige Silus. The first Nexus tower's fallen and T1 are just looking for the prize. They're hungry for it. T1 are the past, the present, and the future. a new the same empire. T1 are your 2024 world champions. And that concludes all of the current winners from every League of Legends World Championship. From the first Summoner Cup winner, Fanatic all the way to T1's most recent win claiming their fifth Summoner Cup. But the question is now, who will be next? Will the LPL continue their rivalry with the LCK, or will another region rise up and steal the spotlight? And the big question every fan asks year after year, will North America ever win the Summoner Cup? Only time will tell, but [...] that will change today. The Crownless are finally king and Invictus Gaming are your 2018 World Champions. The season 9 World Championship finals took place in 2019 in Paris, France, featuring a total of 24 different teams from around the world. evidences.title: All LoL Worlds winners throughout the years - esports.gg
evidences.content: ## Worlds 2022 The Chase Center in San Francisco was a special place for the Koreans DRX in 2022. More than 70% of the fans, according to Riot, thought that T1 was going to win the Worlds 2022. However, when DRX was losing 2-0 and everything seemed lost, they did a reverse sweep and won the Worlds by 3-2. The top laner Hwang “Kingen” Seong-hoon was elected the MVP of the event. DRX won the Worlds 2022 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2023 In the baseball stadium, Gocheok Sky Dome in Seoul, T1 took their revenge and finally won the Worlds after many losses throughout the year. Against Weibo Gaming, T1 won by 3-0. The top laner Choi “Zeus” Woo-je was elected the MVP of the Worlds. T1 won the Worlds 2023 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2024 [...] The Korean team DAMWON Gaming (previously DWG KIA, now named Dplus Kia) defeated the Chinese team Suning by 3-1. The MVP was the jungler Kim “Canyon” Geon-bu, nowadays playing for Gen.G. DAMWON Gaming won the Worlds 2020 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2021 The winners of 2020 couldn’t keep the victory for another year in the Worlds. The Chinese team EDward Gaming won against DWG KIA by 3-2. The finals were in Laugardalshöll Sports Cente...

evidences.title: 2025 World Championship
evidences.content: The 2025 World Championship is Riot Games' premier League of Legends event, featuring 17 teams from multiple regions competing for the Summoner's Cup. The tournament uses a knockout stage with single elimination and Fearless Draft.
